title: sphedamnocarpus andersonii
intro: Sphedamnocarpus andersonii, also known as the "Kadamb" or "White Flame", is a flowering plant native to the Indian subcontinent. It is a deciduous tree with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The plant is known to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, Sphedamnocarpus andersonii is prepared as a decoction, powder, or paste, and is used to treat conditions such as cough, sore throat, and digestive problems.
